name: Shakespeare
description: The Bard's plays, characters, and famous lines

questions.q: The famous soliloquy beginning 'To be or not to be' appears in which Shakespeare play?

source.notes: Act 3, Scene 1. Spoken by Prince Hamlet contemplating death.

questions.q: Whose skull does Hamlet famously address in a graveyard scene?

source.notes: 'Alas, poor Yorick! I knew him, Horatio.' The court jester.

questions.q: In 'Hamlet,' how is King Claudius related to Prince Hamlet?

source.notes: Claudius murdered Hamlet's father and married Gertrude, Hamlet's mother.

questions.q: In which country is the play 'Macbeth' set?

source.notes: Based loosely on King Macbeth of Scotland (11th century).

questions.q: Who kills Macbeth at the end of Shakespeare's tragedy?

source.notes: Macduff was 'from his mother's womb untimely ripp'd' (born by cesarean).

questions.q: In Shakespeare's plays, the city of Verona is the setting for 'Romeo and Juliet' and is located in which country?

source.notes: Northern Italy. Also setting of 'Two Gentlemen of Verona.'

questions.q: Who marries Romeo and Juliet in secret?

source.notes: Also provides Juliet with the sleeping potion that goes tragically wrong.

questions.q: What is the name of the Queen of the Fairies in 'A Midsummer Night's Dream'?

source.notes: Her husband is Oberon, King of the Fairies.

questions.q: In 'A Midsummer Night's Dream,' which character has their head magically transformed into that of a donkey?

source.notes: Nick Bottom, a weaver. Titania falls in love with him in this state.

questions.q: Which Shakespeare play contains the famous stage direction 'Exit, pursued by a bear'?

source.notes: Act 3, Scene 3. Antigonus exits and is killed by the bear offstage.

questions.q: In 'Othello,' what military rank does the title character hold?

source.notes: Moor in service of Venice. Full title: 'The Tragedy of Othello, the Moor of Venice.'

questions.q: In Shakespeare's 'Richard III,' what physical feature is attributed to the title character?

source.notes: Likely exaggerated for dramatic effect. Modern skeletal analysis shows he had scoliosis.

questions.q: In what year did William Shakespeare die?

source.notes: Died April 23, 1616 (his birthday, coincidentally). Age 52.

questions.q: Who was Shakespeare's wife, also sharing her name with a famous modern actress?

source.notes: Married 1582 when he was 18, she was 26. Three children.

questions.q: Who is the title character of 'The Merchant of Venice'?

source.notes: Shylock is the moneylender. Antonio is the titular merchant.

questions.q: In Shakespeare's 'King Lear,' Lear is king of which kingdom?

source.notes: Set in a pre-Christian, legendary Britain. Lear divides his kingdom among three daughters.

questions.q: Shakespeare was born and died in which English town?

source.notes: Born in Stratford, worked in London, died in Stratford.

questions.q: Macbeth is given which Scottish title (or 'thanedom') by King Duncan?

source.notes: He was already Thane of Glamis when the play begins.

questions.q: Which of Shakespeare's plays is classified as a 'history,' dealing with the king who gave the St. Crispin's Day speech?

source.notes: 'We few, we happy few, we band of brothers.' Battle of Agincourt 1415.

questions.q: Which of these Shakespeare plays is categorized as a comedy?

source.notes: Also titled 'What You Will.' Features cross-dressing heroine Viola.

questions.q: Which witch prophecy ultimately comes true when a forest seemingly moves against Macbeth?

source.notes: Soldiers carry branches from Birnam Wood to Dunsinane to disguise their numbers.

questions.q: In 'Romeo and Juliet,' what noble title does Paris hold?

source.notes: He is Juliet's intended suitor, arranged by her father.
